description: The layer of gases held by Earth's gravity that surrounds the planet and protects life by absorbing UV radiation, warming the surface, and reducing temperature extremes.

contents.name: totalMassKg
contents.description: Approximate total mass of Earth's atmosphere in kilograms.

contents.name: co2ConcentrationPpm
contents.description: Current atmospheric CO₂ concentration in parts per million.

contents.name: isPartOf
contents.description: The Atmosphere is part of Earth.

contents.name: interactsWith
contents.description: The Atmosphere interacts with the Biosphere through gas exchange, weather, and climate.

description: The lowest layer of Earth's atmosphere, from the surface to approximately 12 km altitude. Contains 75% of atmospheric mass and virtually all weather phenomena.

contents.name: altitudeRangeKm
contents.description: Altitude range of the Troposphere from surface level (0 km) to approximately 12 km.

contents.name: weatherEvent
contents.description: Current weather condition telemetry (e.g. storm, clear, rain).

description: The atmospheric layer above the Troposphere, from approximately 12–50 km altitude. Contains the ozone layer which absorbs and scatters UV solar radiation.

contents.name: altitudeRangeKm
contents.description: Altitude range of the Stratosphere, approximately 12–50 km.

contents.name: ozoneDobsonUnits
contents.description: Thickness of the ozone layer measured in Dobson Units.

description: The atmospheric layer above the Stratosphere, from approximately 50–80 km altitude. The layer where most meteors burn up upon entering Earth's atmosphere.

contents.name: altitudeRangeKm
contents.description: Altitude range of the Mesosphere, approximately 50–80 km.

contents.name: minTemperatureCelsius
contents.description: Minimum temperature recorded in the Mesosphere, typically around -90°C.

description: The atmospheric layer above the Mesosphere, from approximately 80–600 km altitude. Where auroras (Northern and Southern Lights) occur and where the International Space Station orbits.

contents.name: altitudeRangeKm
contents.description: Altitude range of the Thermosphere, approximately 80–600 km.

contents.name: auroraActivity
contents.description: Current aurora activity level (none, low, moderate, high, extreme).

description: The outermost layer of Earth's atmosphere, from approximately 600–10,000 km altitude. Gases gradually thin and merge with interplanetary space. Transitions into Geospace.

contents.name: altitudeRangeKm
contents.description: Altitude range of the Exosphere, approximately 600–10,000 km.

contents.name: transitionsInto
contents.description: The Exosphere gradually transitions into Geospace at its upper boundary.
